mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat, the policy rate path signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor deterioration that overwhelms the discount-rate channel.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in most periods where FEDFUNDS has been falling for at least 2 consecutive months, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening household income and lower expected sales, so equity prices should decline over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid if unemployment rises while payroll growth remains strong and SPX still advances.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect disappears when PAYEMS growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad final-demand strength, improving revenue expectations and supporting higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if retail sales rise only because of inflation while production and employment are flat.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable or lower CPIAUCSL indicates improving real activity without inflation pressure, which supports equity margins and boosts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when industrial output rises because of a temporary restocking rebound that does not translate into earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change stays below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread between high- and low-signal periods is not statistically different at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% in the presence of high CPIAUCSL marks a restrictive monetary regime that compresses valuation multiples and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails when inflation is falling quickly enough that markets price an imminent easing cycle.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 5-day SPX returns occur in more than 40% of triggered tightening observations, or if the interaction term does not outperform a single-FEDFUNDS rule in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining PAYEMS after a recent employment plateau signals slowing nominal demand and weaker earnings momentum, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if payroll softness is fully offset by strong consumer spending and industrial production.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months after prior 3-month stagnation
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are positive in most payroll-decline episodes, or if the sign flips when RSAFS and INDPRO are included as controls.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth turns positive after a prior slowdown, it marks a cyclical reacceleration in aggregate demand that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if GDP improvement is concentrated in inventory noise without concurrent labor or sales improvement.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q annualized growth rises above its prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP upturn episodes do not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS and RSAFS are not simultaneously improving.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime, falling CPIAUCSL combined with steady INDPRO lowers real-rate pressure and supports higher equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect fails if inflation falls because demand is collapsing faster than prices, which should instead hurt equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls below 2.5% while INDPRO is non-declining
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation episodes do not generate positive 5-day SPX returns above baseline, or if the signal becomes negative when INDPRO is contracting.
